content.problemStatement:
Construct an array of length NNN containing only positive integers in the range [1,1000][1, 1000][1,1000] such that there doesn’t exist a subarray that has all elements occurring in even frequency and the maximum element in the array is minimized. In case there are multiple solutions, you can output any.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, one integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line NNN space-separated integers representing the required array. If there are multiple solutions, you may output any.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: [1][1][1] is the obvious answer.
Test case 222: Since we need two elements and they can't be the same (otherwise the frequency of that element of the whole array is even), [2,1][2, 1][2,1] is the optimal answer. [1,2][1, 2][1,2] is another optimal one.
Test case 333: It can be proven that no construction exists with the maximum element of 222.
